stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (the premium version) yields a guaranteed return of $125K. Option B (the budget version) depends on market demand: with probability 34-52% (uncertain), it yields $313K; otherwise it yields $29K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in market demand affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $125K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $125.6K to $176.7K. Point estimate: $151.1K. Option B is preferred at most assumptions (EV_B > EV_A when P(good) > 33.8%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 33.8%, which falls outside the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
